sme_leads.notes: No web presence found — relies entirely on word of mouth and a listing on Yell. A simple site with a portfolio and quote request form would capture homeowners searching 'carpet fitters Southampton' on Google.

Has a basic site but no online booking and no mobile-responsive layout — users on phones see a broken layout. Adding Fresha or Calendly integration and a mobile rebuild would convert casual social media followers into booked appointments.

Visible on Google Maps with decent reviews but zero website. Student area location means heavy mobile search — a landing page with menu, opening hours, and a click-to-order link would immediately lift covers.

Checkatrade profile only — no standalone website. Tradespeople with their own domain and a clear services/testimonials page consistently outrank directory listings in local search. Strong pitch: 'own your Google rank, not just a Checkatrade profile.'

Existing site is built on an old template — no SSL on all pages, tiny text on mobile, and the online booking link is broken. A clean rebuild with working booking and Google reviews integration would reduce front-desk admin calls significantly.

linkedin_prospects.name: Karen Whitfield
linkedin_prospects.title: Branch Manager
linkedin_prospects.reason: Manages a team of 6 negotiators in a competitive Southampton market where conversion from valuation to instruction is the key metric. CloseCoach's objection-handling roleplay maps directly to the 'vendor choosing between agents' call — a daily pressure point for her team.

linkedin_prospects.name: James Colton
linkedin_prospects.title: Sales Manager
linkedin_prospects.reason: Automotive sales managers are under constant pressure on monthly plate targets; finance upsell and test-drive close rates are the two biggest skill gaps. CloseCoach can simulate real buyer objections so reps practise without burning live prospects.

linkedin_prospects.name: Sarah Nwosu
linkedin_prospects.title: Head of Sales
linkedin_prospects.reason: Heads a 10-person permanent recruitment desk with high staff turnover and inconsistent billing across the team. CloseCoach gives her a way to upskill new consultants on BD calls and client retainer conversations without pulling senior billers off their desks.

dts.title: "I swapped my driving test and passed 3 months earlier — here's exactly how"
dts.format: 60-second talking-head from a real or dramatised DTS user walking through finding an earlier slot, the nerves of the swap, and passing sooner — end with the DTS URL as CTA.

dts.title: "DVSA test wait times by area — April 2026 update (some are WILD)"
dts.format: Screen-record scrolling through the DVSA booking system showing extreme wait times in major cities, cut with on-screen text reactions — positioned as proof that swapping is necessary, not just convenient.

athletics.title: "Why your sprint isn't getting faster (it's not your legs)"
athletics.format: Dowayne talks to camera on a track breaking down one underrated sprint mechanic (e.g. arm drive or hip height), demonstrated with a before/after clip of the same drill done wrong vs. right.

athletics.title: "Day in the life: training around a full work schedule"
athletics.format: Quick-cut vlog of a real training session squeezed around a busy day — morning session, work tasks, evening cool-down. Honest and relatable, positions Dowayne as building discipline not just showcasing peak performance.

aimybiz.title: "Most sales reps can't handle this 1 objection — watch AI coach them through it in real time"
aimybiz.format: Screen-capture of a CloseCoach live roleplay where the AI buyer pushes back hard on price — show the rep fumbling, then coached and nailing the reframe. Product demo that doubles as insight content.

aimybiz.title: "I built an AI that will argue with your sales team. Here's what we learned after 100 practice calls."
aimybiz.format: Long-form post in Dowayne's voice sharing 3 patterns CloseCoach exposed (e.g. reps giving up too early on follow-up, over-explaining price). Ends with a soft CTA to trial CloseCoach — designed to pull comments from sales managers.
